title: The Eighteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
September 23, 2018 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Eighteenth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time (the longest liturgical season). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have been Jeremiah 11:4-20 (or Amos 8:4-7), Psalm 54, James 3:16-4:6, and Mark 9:30-37, with the Gospel emphasizing Jesus teaching about humility and receiving children. The liturgical color would be green, reflecting the continuation of Ordinary Time without special festival character.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the interim period between General Conventions, with the 79th General Convention having concluded in Austin, Texas in July 2018. The church was navigating ongoing debates over human sexuality and biblical interpretation that had dominated the convention. In the Diocese of Colorado, Bishop Robert O'Neill was serving as the ordinary, having been elected in 2012; no major diocesan transitions were occurring at this specific moment.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early fall weather with temperatures in the 70s-80s Fahrenheit. The immediate prior week had seen Colorado political news focused on the midterm election cycle heating up, with Senate race coverage between incumbent Democrat Michael Bennet and Republican challengers intensifying. The Cathedral sits in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, which was experiencing ongoing gentrification and development pressures, typical of the period's Denver real estate boom.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The nation was consumed by the Brett Kavanaugh Supreme Court confirmation hearings, which had intensified dramatically on September 27 with Christine Blasey Ford's testimony (just days after this sermon), creating an unprecedented national division. The Trump administration was continuing its trade war with China, with tariffs set to expand. The ongoing migrant caravan from Central America remained a flashpoint in political and media discourse, with the administration focusing heavily on border security messaging ahead of midterms.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Turkish government was in a severe currency and economic crisis, with the Turkish lira having collapsed to record lows against the dollar in August 2018, creating regional economic instability. Italy's new populist government was confronting the European Union over budgetary demands and migration policy, signaling fractures in European unity. Thailand's King Maha Vajiralongkorn had recently ascended following the death of his father, with coronation ceremonies being prepared.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The prevailing mood in America was polarized anxiety heading into the midterm elections, with intense partisan division and questions about institutional legitimacy. Sports culture was dominated by the NFL anthem controversy continuing from 2016, now a symbol of broader social division. The #MeToo movement remained culturally central, with the Kavanaugh hearings immediately about to become the year's defining moment on gender and power dynamics.
